spec_match.rationale: The concept uses the actual vertical and horizontal scroll containers as named scroll-state query containers and demonstrates directional scrollable states.

interactive.rationale: Range controls, overflow toggles, scroll-position buttons, and live badges change the content, overflow, and scroll state in page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now delivers the fade-indicator behavior promised by the feature index for top, bottom, left, and right overflow hints.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers no-overflow, overflow-hidden, start, middle, end, horizontal, vertical, and unsupported-browser fallback states.

references.rationale: The page includes the required chromestatus.com feature link and the CSS Conditional Rules reference.

design_system.rationale: Inline CSS uses shared design-system variables, avoids raw color literals, and pairs the active button background with an explicit text color.

summary: The previous static fade example has been replaced with an interactive scroll-state lab that exercises real scroll containers, controls state changes, reports live computed state, and degrades cleanly when CSS scroll-state queries are unavailable.
